1	package agent
2	
3	// provider_failure.go defines the typed provider-failure taxonomy at the
4	// DDx/Fizeau boundary (ddx-3b721804). A provider failure is any pre-dispatch
5	// Execute error or failed final event whose root cause is provider/model
6	// availability, credentials, quota/rate limiting, endpoint reachability, or
7	// harness/configuration validity — as opposed to a model giving up on the task.
8	//
9	// DDx is the abstraction that lets unattended workers continue through provider
10	// variance across machines. A generic, untyped provider failure that names no
11	// cause, no fallback action, and no route-health evidence is a product bug, so
12	// this file gives every such failure a typed reason, a retryability verdict, a
13	// fallback decision, and durable evidence.
14	//
15	// Routing itself stays in Fizeau (aliases, model discovery, provider catalog);
16	// DDx only classifies the outcome and decides whether an unpinned worker should
17	// fall back to another eligible route or a pinned worker should fail loudly.
18	
19	import (
20		"errors"
21		"strings"
22	
23		agentlib "github.com/easel/fizeau"
24	)
25	
26	// Provider-failure taxonomy. provider_connectivity and no_viable_provider reuse
27	// the existing FailureMode* constants (execute_bead_status.go) so the rest of
28	// the loop's route-health handling continues to recognize them.
29	const (
30		FailureModeProviderAuth               = "provider_auth"
31		FailureModeProviderRateLimit          = "provider_rate_limit"
32		FailureModeProviderQuota              = "provider_quota"
33		FailureModeProviderModelUnavailable   = "provider_model_unavailable"
34		FailureModeProviderHarnessUnavailable = "provider_harness_unavailable"
35		FailureModeProviderConfigInvalid      = "provider_config_invalid"
36		FailureModeUnknownProviderFailure     = "unknown_provider_failure"
37	)
38	
39	// ProviderFailure is the typed classification of a provider/route failure.
40	type ProviderFailure struct {
41		// Reason is one of the taxonomy constants above (or the reused
42		// FailureModeProviderConnectivity / FailureModeNoViableProvider).
43		Reason string
44		// Retryable reports whether a *different* eligible route could succeed.
45		// True for transient/availability failures an unpinned worker should fall
46		// back from; false for whole-fleet conditions (no viable provider) or
47		// configuration bugs that another route cannot fix.
48		Retryable bool
49		// Disruption is the disruption_reason stamped on the report. A provider
50		// failure is always a worker-side disruption (the model never got to give
51		// up), so it mirrors Reason.
52		Disruption string
53	}
54	
55	// ProviderFailureError wraps a pre-dispatch Execute error with its typed
56	// classification so callers can recover the taxonomy via errors.As without
57	// re-parsing free text. The wrapped error's message is preserved verbatim.
58	type ProviderFailureError struct {
59		Failure ProviderFailure
60		Err     error
61	}
62	
63	func (e *ProviderFailureError) Error() string {
64		if e == nil || e.Err == nil {
65			return ""
66		}
67		return e.Err.Error()
68	}
69	
70	func (e *ProviderFailureError) Unwrap() error {
71		if e == nil {
72			return nil
73		}
74		return e.Err
75	}
76	
77	// ClassifyProviderFailure normalizes a free-text provider/route error into the
78	// typed taxonomy. It returns ok=false when the text does not name a recognized
79	// provider/route/config cause, so callers can leave non-provider failures
80	// (test_failure, merge_conflict, ...) classified by ClassifyFailureMode.
81	//
82	// The order is significant: config-invalid and auth/quota/rate-limit markers
83	// are checked before the broad connectivity bucket so a "429" or "unauthorized"
84	// is not swallowed as a generic timeout.
85	func ClassifyProviderFailure(errMsg string) (ProviderFailure, bool) {
86		lower := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(errMsg))
87		if lower == "" {
88			return ProviderFailure{}, false
89		}
90		switch {
91		case containsAny(lower,
92			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
93			"passthrough constraint:",
94			"invalid configuration",
95			"config invalid",
96			"invalid endpoint",
97			"misconfigured",
98			"unknown provider",
99			"harness model incompatible",
100			"model incompatible with",
101			"incompatible with power bounds"):
102			return providerFailure(FailureModeProviderConfigInvalid, false), true
103		case containsAny(lower,
104			"unauthorized",
105			"401",
106			"invalid api key",
107			"invalid_api_key",
108			"authentication failed",
109			"authentication_error",
110			"no api key",
111			"missing api key",
112			"forbidden",
113			"403"):
114			return providerFailure(FailureModeProviderAuth, true), true
115		case containsAny(lower,
116			"quota exceeded",
117			"insufficient quota",
118			"insufficient_quota",
119			"out of quota",
120			"quota"):
121			return providerFailure(FailureModeProviderQuota, true), true
122		case containsAny(lower,
123			"rate limit",
124			"rate_limit",
125			"ratelimit",
126			"too many requests",
127			"429"):
128			return providerFailure(FailureModeProviderRateLimit, true), true
129		case containsAny(lower, "is not routable", "not routable", "model_not_found"),
130			strings.Contains(lower, "model") && containsAny(lower,
131				"not available",
132				"unavailable",
133				"not found",
134				"no such",
135				"unknown",
136				"incompatible"):
137			return providerFailure(FailureModeProviderModelUnavailable, true), true
138		case containsAny(lower,
139			"harness unavailable",
140			"harness not available",
141			"harness not installed",
142			"unknown harness",
143			"no harness configured",
144			"executable file not found",
145			"command not found"):
146			return providerFailure(FailureModeProviderHarnessUnavailable, true), true
147		case containsAny(lower,
148			"no viable provider",
149			"no viable harness",
150			"no viable routing candidate",
151			"no live provider supports",
152			"no candidate satisfying local endpoint"):
153			return providerFailure(FailureModeNoViableProvider, false), true
154		case containsAny(lower,
155			"connection refused",
156			"connection reset",
157			"no route to host",
158			"network is unreachable",
159			"no such host",
160			"i/o timeout",
161			"dial tcp",
162			"provider_connectivity",
163			"provider request timeout",
164			"provider timeout",
165			"endpoint timeout",
166			"bad gateway",
167			"service unavailable",
168			"gateway timeout",
169			"502", "503", "504"):
170			return providerFailure(FailureModeProviderConnectivity, true), true
171		}
172		return ProviderFailure{}, false
173	}
174	
175	// ClassifyServiceExecuteError classifies a pre-dispatch FizeauService.Execute
176	// error. Because a pre-dispatch failure means routing never produced a viable
177	// dispatch, every such error is a provider-boundary failure: when no specific
178	// marker matches it falls back to unknown_provider_failure (non-retryable) so
179	// the outcome is always typed rather than generic execution_failed.
180	func ClassifyServiceExecuteError(err error) ProviderFailure {
181		if err == nil {
182			return providerFailure(FailureModeUnknownProviderFailure, false)
183		}
184		var modelErr *agentlib.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
185		if errors.As(err, &modelErr) {
186			return providerFailure(FailureModeProviderModelUnavailable, true)
187		}
188		if pf, ok := ClassifyProviderFailure(err.Error()); ok {
189			return pf
190		}
191		return providerFailure(FailureModeUnknownProviderFailure, false)
192	}
193	
194	// ApplyProviderFailureToReport stamps a typed provider failure onto a report:
195	// the typed reason as outcome_reason and the worker-disruption markers so the
196	// retry/fallback layer treats it as route-health evidence, not a model give-up.
197	func ApplyProviderFailureToReport(report *ExecuteBeadReport, pf ProviderFailure) {
198		if report == nil || pf.Reason == "" {
199			return
200		}
201		report.OutcomeReason = pf.Reason
202		report.Disrupted = true
203		report.DisruptionReason = pf.Disruption
204	}
205	
206	// IsProviderFailureReason reports whether reason is one of the typed
207	// provider-failure taxonomy values (including the reused connectivity /
208	// no-viable-provider reasons).
209	func IsProviderFailureReason(reason string) bool {
210		switch strings.TrimSpace(reason) {
211		case FailureModeProviderAuth,
212			FailureModeProviderRateLimit,
213			FailureModeProviderQuota,
214			FailureModeProviderModelUnavailable,
215			FailureModeProviderHarnessUnavailable,
216			FailureModeProviderConfigInvalid,
217			FailureModeUnknownProviderFailure,
218			FailureModeProviderConnectivity,
219			FailureModeNoViableProvider:
220			return true
221		default:
222			return false
223		}
224	}
225	
226	// ProviderPin captures an operator's explicit routing pins. A pinned worker
227	// must never have its pin silently widened: a typed provider failure on a
228	// pinned route is reported as hard-pin-exhausted with operator remediation.
229	type ProviderPin struct {
230		Harness  string
231		Provider string
232		Model    string
233	}
234	
235	// Any reports whether the operator pinned any routing dimension.
236	func (p ProviderPin) Any() bool {
237		return strings.TrimSpace(p.Harness) != "" ||
238			strings.TrimSpace(p.Provider) != "" ||
239			strings.TrimSpace(p.Model) != ""
240	}
241	
242	func (p ProviderPin) describe() string {
243		var parts []string
244		if h := strings.TrimSpace(p.Harness); h != "" {
245			parts = append(parts, "--harness "+h)
246		}
247		if pr := strings.TrimSpace(p.Provider); pr != "" {
248			parts = append(parts, "--provider "+pr)
249		}
250		if m := strings.TrimSpace(p.Model); m != "" {
251			parts = append(parts, "--model "+m)
252		}
253		return strings.Join(parts, " ")
254	}
255	
256	// FallbackDecision records whether the worker should attempt another route and,
257	// when it should not, why fallback stopped. The StopReason feeds durable
258	// evidence (fallback_stop_reason).
259	type FallbackDecision struct {
260		Continue   bool
261		StopReason string
262	}
263	
264	// Fallback stop reasons used in evidence.
265	const (
266		FallbackStopHardPinExhausted = "hard_pin_exhausted"
267	)
268	
269	// DecideProviderFallback decides what an worker does after a typed provider
270	// failure. A pinned worker never widens its pin, so any provider failure stops
271	// with hard_pin_exhausted. An unpinned worker continues when the failure is
272	// retryable (a different eligible route may succeed) and otherwise stops naming
273	// the typed reason (e.g. no_viable_provider — nothing left to try).
274	func DecideProviderFallback(pf ProviderFailure, pinned bool) FallbackDecision {
275		if pinned {
276			return FallbackDecision{Continue: false, StopReason: FallbackStopHardPinExhausted}
277		}
278		if pf.Retryable {
279			return FallbackDecision{Continue: true}
280		}
281		return FallbackDecision{Continue: false, StopReason: pf.Reason}
282	}
283	
284	// ProviderFailureEvidence is the durable record persisted on the bead/run when
285	// a provider failure occurs. It proves what was requested, what (if anything)
286	// resolved, the typed failure, its retryability, whether fallback was
287	// attempted, and why fallback stopped — so an operator reading the bead can see
288	// the full route-health decision without re-running the worker.
289	type ProviderFailureEvidence struct {
290		RequestedHarness   string `json:"requested_harness,omitempty"`
291		RequestedProvider  string `json:"requested_provider,omitempty"`
292		RequestedModel     string `json:"requested_model,omitempty"`
293		RequestedProfile   string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
294		RequestedMinPower  int    `json:"requested_min_power,omitempty"`
295		RequestedMaxPower  int    `json:"requested_max_power,omitempty"`
296		ResolvedHarness    string `json:"resolved_harness,omitempty"`
297		ResolvedProvider   string `json:"resolved_provider,omitempty"`
298		ResolvedModel      string `json:"resolved_model,omitempty"`
299		TypedFailure       string `json:"typed_failure"`
300		Retryable          bool   `json:"retryable"`
301		FallbackAttempted  bool   `json:"fallback_attempted"`
302		FallbackStopReason string `json:"fallback_stop_reason,omitempty"`
303	}
304	
305	// ProviderFailureRequest captures the constraints the worker requested for the
306	// failed dispatch.
307	type ProviderFailureRequest struct {
308		Harness  string
309		Provider string
310		Model    string
311		Profile  string
312		MinPower int
313		MaxPower int
314	}
315	
316	// ResolvedRoute captures the route Fizeau resolved, when any route resolved
317	// before the failure. Nil/zero means routing never produced a candidate.
318	type ResolvedRoute struct {
319		Harness  string
320		Provider string
321		Model    string
322	}
323	
324	// BuildProviderFailureEvidence assembles the durable evidence for a provider
325	// failure from the requested constraints, the resolved route (if any), the
326	// typed failure, and the fallback decision.
327	func BuildProviderFailureEvidence(req ProviderFailureRequest, resolved *ResolvedRoute, pf ProviderFailure, decision FallbackDecision) ProviderFailureEvidence {
328		ev := ProviderFailureEvidence{
329			RequestedHarness:   req.Harness,
330			RequestedProvider:  req.Provider,
331			RequestedModel:     req.Model,
332			RequestedProfile:   req.Profile,
333			RequestedMinPower:  req.MinPower,
334			RequestedMaxPower:  req.MaxPower,
335			TypedFailure:       pf.Reason,
336			Retryable:          pf.Retryable,
337			FallbackAttempted:  decision.Continue,
338			FallbackStopReason: decision.StopReason,
339		}
340		if resolved != nil {
341			ev.ResolvedHarness = resolved.Harness
342			ev.ResolvedProvider = resolved.Provider
343			ev.ResolvedModel = resolved.Model
344		}
345		return ev
346	}
347	
348	// MarkHardPinExhausted refines a report for a pinned worker whose pinned route
349	// hit a typed provider failure. It preserves the pin (it never clears the pin
350	// or widens routing), records the typed failure as the outcome reason, and
351	// writes operator remediation naming the exact pin so the operator can act.
352	func MarkHardPinExhausted(report *ExecuteBeadReport, pin ProviderPin, pf ProviderFailure) {
353		if report == nil || pf.Reason == "" {
354			return
355		}
356		report.OutcomeReason = pf.Reason
357		report.Disrupted = true
358		report.DisruptionReason = pf.Disruption
359		remediation := "hard pin exhausted: typed provider failure " + pf.Reason +
360			" on pinned route (" + pin.describe() + "); the pin was not widened. " +
361			"Remediation: fix the pinned provider/model/harness, or rerun without the pin to let DDx fall back to another eligible route."
362		if strings.TrimSpace(report.Detail) == "" {
363			report.Detail = remediation
364		} else {
365			report.Detail = report.Detail + "; " + remediation
366		}
367	}
368	
369	func providerFailure(reason string, retryable bool) ProviderFailure {
370		return ProviderFailure{Reason: reason, Retryable: retryable, Disruption: reason}
371	}

input.prompt:
I'm working in a Go CLI repo at /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c02ce93c-20260613T225213-a01c938c (the `cli/` subdir is the Go module). I need to understand how `ddx work` workers, attempts, and worker status are structured, and how provider/agent subprocesses are spawned and managed. Please do a thorough exploration and report back with file:line references.

Specifically find and report:

1. The `ddx work` command implementation — where is it (cmd/), and where is the worker loop / attempt lifecycle? Look for "attempt", "worker", process management.

2. `ddx work status` (or `ddx work --status` / `work status --json`) — where is worker status reported as JSON? What struct represents the worker status / attempt status? Find the JSON-serializable status struct(s) and their fields. The bead AC wants to add a `provider_children` field showing pid, provider/harness, route_owner, phase, age.

3. How are agent/provider subprocesses actually spawned? The agent runs via `github.com/easel/fizeau` (agentlib) `svc.Execute(ctx, req)`. Is there any place in DDx where os/exec is used to spawn child processes directly? Search for `exec.Command`, `exec.CommandContext`, `SysProcAttr`, `Setpgid`, process groups, `syscall.Kill`, signal handling in the agent and work packages.

4. Where does an attempt end / get cleaned up? Look for cleanup, defer, signal handling, cancellation, timeout handling in the work loop and agent runner.

5. Any existing process tracking, child-process, or pid-tracking code anywhere in cli/internal/agent/ or cli/internal/server/ or cli/cmd/.

6. The structure of cli/internal/agent/ package — list the .go files and a one-line summary of each that's relevant to worker execution, attempts, status, and process management.

7. How is worker status persisted/read? Is there a status file, an in-memory server, an IPC mechanism? The `ddx work status --json` must read it from somewhere.

Report concrete file paths and line numbers. Do not modify anything.

17	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
18	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
19	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
20	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
21	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
22	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:           "work",
25			Short:         "Drain the bead execution queue",
26			SilenceUsage:  true,
27			SilenceErrors: true,
28			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
29	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
30	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
31	
32	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
33	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
34	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
35	bounds.
36	
37	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
38	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
39	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
40	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
41	
42	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
43	  drained     — no ready beads remain
44	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
45	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
46	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
47	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
48	
49	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
50	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
51	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
52	`,
53			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
54	  ddx work
55	
56	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
57	  ddx work --once
58	
59	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
60	  ddx work --watch
61	
62	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
63	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
64	
65	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
66	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
67	
68	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
69	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
70	
71	  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
72	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
73			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
74			RunE: f.runWork,
75		}
76	
77		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
78		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
79		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
80		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
81		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
82		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
83		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
84		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
85		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
86		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-backend", "", "Attempt backend: worktree, local-clone, docker-clone, or in-tree (default: executions.attempt_backend)")
87		cmd.Flags().Bool("ignore-cooldown", false, "Ignore retry cooldown across this worker session (requires --reason)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("reason", "", "Operator reason required by --ignore-cooldown")
89		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
90		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
91		cmd.Flags().Bool("self-refresh", false, "Force re-exec onto a newer installed ddx binary between iterations (on by default in watch mode; use --no-self-refresh to disable)")
92		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-self-refresh", false, "Disable self-refresh: do NOT re-exec onto a newer installed ddx binary between watch iterations (self-refresh is on by default in watch mode)")
93		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
94		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
95		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
96		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
97		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
99		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
100		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart powerClass)")
101		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
102		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-bead-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxBeadCostUSD, "Per-bead cost budget in USD; stop escalating when this bead's billed cost exceeds this amount (0 = unlimited); overridden per-bead by a budget:<USD> label")
103		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-recovery-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxRecoveryCostUSD, "Per-bead automated recovery budget in USD for reframe/decompose attempts after repeated ladder exhaustion")
104		cmd.Flags().Int("claim-rate-window", agent.DefaultClaimSuccessRateWindow, "Rolling window of recent claim attempts used to compute the claim success rate warning")
105		cmd.Flags().Float64("claim-rate-threshold", agent.DefaultClaimSuccessRateThreshold, "Warn when the rolling claim success rate over a full window is at or below this threshold")
106		cmd.Flags().Int("preclaim-warn-threshold", agent.DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold, "Consecutive identical pre-claim warn fingerprints across distinct bead IDs required before the loop emits operator attention")
107		cmd.Flags().Duration("preclaim-timeout", workguard.DefaultPreClaimTimeout, "Pre-claim readiness timeout for preflight/readiness hooks")
108		cmd.Flags().Duration("route-resolution-timeout", agent.DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout, "Timeout bounding routing preflight and the resolveRoute viability check; on expiry the lease is released and the bead is flagged for operator attention")
109		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
110		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
111		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
112			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
113		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
114		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
115		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
116		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
117	
118		// Register "ddx work plan", "ddx work focus", "ddx work clear-cooldowns",
119		// "ddx work metrics", "ddx work analyze", and "ddx work status" as
120		// subcommands.
121		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
122		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
123		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkClearCooldownsCommand())
124		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkMetricsCommand())
125		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkAnalyzeCommand())
126		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkStatusCommand())
127	
128		return cmd
129	}

131	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
132	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
133	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
134	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
135	// by this layer.
136	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
137		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
138			return err
139		}
140	
141		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
142		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
143		lockmetrics.SetSink(lockmetrics.FileSink(projectRoot))
144		lockmetrics.SetCapEnforcement(projectRoot, "")
145		f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)
146	
147		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
148		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
149		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
150		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
151		if !asJSON {
152			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
153			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
154				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
155			})
156		}
157	
158		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
159	}

161	func writeExecuteLoopResult(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult, asJSON bool) error {
162		if asJSON {
163			payload := struct {
164				ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
165				*agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult
166			}{
167				ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
168				ExecuteBeadLoopResult: result,
169			}
170			enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
171			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
172			return enc.Encode(payload)
173		}
174	
175		if result.NoReadyWork && result.Attempts == 0 {
176			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, projectRoot, result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
177			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
178			return nil
179		}
180	
181		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
182		writeWorkTerminalSummary(w, result)
183		writeOperatorAttentionSummary(w, result.OperatorAttention)
184		preserved := preservedWorkAttempts(result)
185		if len(preserved) > 0 {
186			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\npreserved:\n")
187			for _, attempt := range preserved {
188				fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
189				if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
190					fmt.Fprintf(w, " (%s)", attempt.PreserveRef)
191				}
192				fmt.Fprintln(w)
193			}
194		}
195		failed := failedWorkAttempts(result)
196		if len(failed) > 0 {
197			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nfailed:\n")
198			for _, attempt := range failed {
199				fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
200				if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
201					fmt.Fprintf(w, " (preserved)")
202				}
203				fmt.Fprintln(w)
204			}
205		}
206		if result.NoReadyWork {
207			fmt.Fprintln(w)
208			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, "", result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
209			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
210		}
211		return nil
212	}

241	func countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) (closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, preserved int) {
242		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
243			switch attempt.Status {
244			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
245				closed++
246				changed++
247			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
248				closed++
249				alreadySatisfied++
250			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview:
251				preserved++
252			}
253		}
254		if closed == 0 && result.Successes > 0 {
255			closed = result.Successes
256		}
257		return closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, preserved
258	}

260	func preservedWorkAttempts(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) []agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
261		if result == nil {
262			return nil
263		}
264		var out []agent.ExecuteBeadReport
265		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
266			if attempt.Status == agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview {
267				out = append(out, attempt)
268			}
269		}
270		return out
271	}
272	
273	func failedWorkAttempts(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) []agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
274		if result == nil {
275			return nil
276		}
277		var out []agent.ExecuteBeadReport
278		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
279			switch attempt.Status {
280			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
281				agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied,
282				agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview:
283				continue
284			default:
285				out = append(out, attempt)
286			}
287		}
288		return out
289	}

291	func workExitSummary(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) string {
292		switch result.ExitReason {
293		case "drained":
294			return "drained current execution-ready queue"
295		case "once_complete":
296			return "once complete"
297		case "budget":
298			return "budget reached"
299		case "no_progress":
300			return "no-progress policy stopped work"
301		case "blocked":
302			return "blocked waiting for external action"
303		case "operator_attention":
304			return "operator attention required"
305		case "sigint", "sigterm", "context_cancelled":
306			return "stopped by signal"
307		case "fatal_config":
308			return "fatal configuration error"
309		case "preflight_failed":
310			return "preflight failed"
311		case "resource_exhausted":
312			return "resource exhausted"
313		case "routing_unavailable":
314			return "routing unavailable"
315		}
316		if result.StopCondition != "" {
317			return strings.ToLower(strings.ReplaceAll(result.StopCondition, "_", " "))
318		}
319		return "completed"
320	}

13	
14	// drainWatchdog carries the three wedge-prevention mechanisms threaded into
15	// drainServiceEventsWithRenderer. All fields are optional: nil/zero disables
16	// the corresponding check.
17	type drainWatchdog struct {
18		// cancel is called when a wedge condition is detected. Must not be nil
19		// when idleTimeout or toolCallTimeout is non-zero.
20		cancel func()
21		// idleTimeout is the window of complete event silence before the execution
22		// is considered wedged and cancelled. Any received event resets this timer;
23		// only true silence (no events of any kind) triggers cancellation.
24		idleTimeout time.Duration
25		// toolCallTimeout is a per-tool-call sanity cap: if no tool_result arrives
26		// within this window after a tool_call, the execution is cancelled. This
27		// catches individually hung subprocesses, not loops (loopDetector handles
28		// loops).
29		toolCallTimeout time.Duration
30	}
31	
32	// loopDetector maintains a window of the last 8 (tool_call, tool_result) pair
33	// keys and fires when the same key appears ≥4 times — indicating the model is
34	// repeating an unproductive command without progress.
35	type loopDetector struct {
36		entries []string // capacity-capped at 8
37	}
38	
39	// record adds key to the window and returns true if a loop is detected.
40	func (d *loopDetector) record(key string) bool {
41		d.entries = append(d.entries, key)
42		if len(d.entries) > 8 {
43			d.entries = d.entries[len(d.entries)-8:]
44		}
45		counts := make(map[string]int, len(d.entries))
46		for _, k := range d.entries {
47			counts[k]++
48			if counts[k] >= 4 {
49				return true
50			}
51		}
52		return false
53	}
54	
55	// makeLoopKey builds a canonical key for a (tool_call, tool_result) pair.
56	// We use tool name + raw input JSON + first 256B of output + error prefix so
57	// that identical unproductive commands are detected regardless of surrounding
58	// whitespace or token formatting.
59	func makeLoopKey(call agentlib.ServiceToolCallData, result agentlib.ServiceToolResultData) string {
60		input := strings.TrimSpace(string(call.Input))
61		out := result.Output
62		if len(out) > 256 {
63			out = out[:256]
64		}
65		errStr := result.Error
66		if len(errStr) > 64 {
67			errStr = errStr[:64]
68		}
69		return call.Name + "\x00" + input + "\x00" + errStr + "\x00" + out
70	}
71	
72	// runAgentViaService is the new RunAgent dispatch path that drives the
73	// agent through agentlib.FizeauService.Execute and drains the resulting event
74	// channel into a DDx Result. Old in-package code paths (RunAgent legacy
75	// loop, embeddedCompactionConfig, buildAgentProvider, findTool, and
76	// wrapProviderWithDeadlines) stay in place; this function does NOT call them.
77	//
78	// Stall detection: we delegate to the agent's StallPolicy. The agent
79	// emits a stall event then a final event with Status="stalled".
80	func runAgentViaService(r *Runner, opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
81		promptText, err := r.resolvePrompt(opts)
82		if err != nil {
83			return nil, err
84		}
85	
86		model := r.resolveModel(opts, "agent")
87		timeout := r.resolveTimeout(opts)
88		wallClock := r.resolveWallClock(opts)
89	
90		wd := opts.WorkDir
91		if wd == "" {
92			wd, _ = os.Getwd()
93		}
94	
95		// Construct the service. Reuses NewServiceFromWorkDir so provider/model
96		// routing data lands on the agent the same way every other DDx command
97		// constructs it (see serviceconfig.go).
98		svc, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(wd)
99		if err != nil {
100			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
101		}
102	
103		// Resolve where to write the per-request session log.
104		logDir := opts.SessionLogDir
105		if logDir == "" {
106			logDir = r.Config.SessionLogDir
107		}
108		if logDir == "" {
109			logDir = DefaultLogDir
110		}
111	
112		providerTimeout := ResolveProviderRequestTimeout(wd, opts.Provider, model, 0)
113	
114		// Build the public ExecuteRequest per CONTRACT-003.
115		req := agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest{
116			Prompt:          promptText,
117			Model:           model,
118			Provider:        opts.Provider,
119			Harness:         "fiz",
120			Reasoning:       agentlib.Reasoning(opts.Effort),
121			Permissions:     opts.Permissions,
122			WorkDir:         wd,
123			Timeout:         wallClock,
124			IdleTimeout:     timeout,
125			ProviderTimeout: providerTimeout,
126			SessionLogDir:   logDir,
127			Metadata:        metadataWithEnv(opts.Correlation, opts.Env),
128			Role:            opts.Role,
129			CorrelationID:   opts.CorrelationID,
130		}
131	
132		parentCtx := opts.Context
133		if parentCtx == nil {
134			parentCtx = context.Background()
135		}
136		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(parentCtx)
137		defer cancel()
138

4	
5	import (
6		"os/exec"
7		"syscall"
8	)
9	
10	// cmdSetProcessGroup configures cmd so that it and all its children share a
11	// new process group (Setpgid) and are automatically killed when the parent
12	// thread dies (Pdeathsig). This ensures that if the parent worker dies
13	// abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM), the harness child is reaped by the kernel
14	// rather than orphaning to PID 1.
15	//
16	// Pdeathsig is sent when the creating thread dies, not the process, so the
17	// caller must use runtime.LockOSThread around cmd.Start() to prevent Go
18	// runtime from migrating the goroutine to a different OS thread. See
19	// golang.org/issue/27505 for details.
20	//
21	// Note: Pdeathsig is Linux-only. macOS and Windows do not support it; the
22	// graceful shutdown path (cmdKillProcessGroup on context cancellation) serves
23	// as the fallback for those platforms, and the orphan reaper is the final
24	// backstop for already-dead workers.
25	func cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
26		cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{
27			Setpgid:   true,
28			Pdeathsig: syscall.SIGKILL,
29		}
30	}
31	
32	// cmdKillProcessGroup sends SIGKILL to the entire process group of cmd.
33	// Because cmd was started with Setpgid=true, its PID equals its PGID, so
34	// passing -pid kills all descendants that inherited the group.
35	func cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
36		if cmd.Process == nil {
37			return
38		}
39		_ = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
40	}

8	)
9	
10	// isPIDAlive reports whether a process with the given PID is alive.
11	// Returns false if pid <= 0 or if the process does not exist (ESRCH).
12	// A zombie process (which has exited but not been waited) returns true
13	// because its PID slot is still held; the caller treats it as dead for
14	// prune purposes only when combined with an age check.
15	func isPIDAlive(pid int) bool {
16		if pid <= 0 {
17			return false
18		}
19		return syscall.Kill(pid, 0) != syscall.ESRCH
20	}
21	
22	// terminateProcessGroup sends SIGTERM to the worker's process group; if the
23	// process is still alive after grace, follows up with SIGKILL. The negative
24	// pid argument to syscall.Kill targets the whole process group, which is
25	// essential for workers that fork child harnesses.
26	//
27	// Callers set Setpgid=true when spawning the process so pid == pgid and the
28	// group id matches. If the caller did not set a new pgid, a negative-pid
29	// signal still works because the worker's own pgid will be the caller's pgid
30	// — not desirable, so the caller contract is: only register a PID for
31	// processes you spawned with their own pgid.
32	func terminateProcessGroup(pid int, grace time.Duration) {
33		if pid <= 0 {
34			return
35		}
36		// SIGTERM to the process group.
37		_ = syscall.Kill(-pid, syscall.SIGTERM)
38	
39		// Poll until grace expires for the leader to exit; then SIGKILL.
40		deadline := time.Now().Add(grace)
41		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
42			if syscall.Kill(pid, 0) == syscall.ESRCH {
43				return
44			}
45			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
46		}
47		_ = syscall.Kill(-pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
48	}

183	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
184		mode := r.Mode
185		if mode == "" {
186			if r.Once {
187				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
188			} else {
189				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
190			}
191		}
192		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
193		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
194			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
195		}
196		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
197			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
198		}
199		return mode, idleInterval
200	}

200	}

363	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
364	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
365	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
366	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
367	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
368	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
369	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
370	//
371	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
372	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
373	//
374	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
375	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
376	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
377	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
378	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
379	// directly.
380	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
381		ctx context.Context,
382		projectRoot string,
383		b *bead.Bead,
384		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
385		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
386		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
387		actor, source string,
388		now func() time.Time,
389	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
390		if now == nil {
391			now = time.Now
392		}
393		if submit == nil {
394			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
395		}
396		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
397		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
398		if err != nil {
399			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
400			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
401			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
402			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
403			if upErr := (&RealGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
404				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
405			}
406			return &LandResult{
407				Status:      "preserved",
408				PreserveRef: ref,
409				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
410			}, nil, nil
411		}
412		if outcome.Blocked {
413			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealGitOps{})
414			if perr != nil {
415				return nil, outcome, perr
416			}
417			if eventStore != nil {
418				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
419			}
420			return land, outcome, nil
421		}
422		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
423			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
424		}
425		var submitted *LandResult
426		cfg, cfgErr := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
427		_, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, &RealGitOps{}, BeadLandingOptions{
428			LandingAdvancer: func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
429				req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res)
430				if cfgErr == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Git != nil && len(cfg.Git.PostLandCommand) > 0 {
431					req.PostLandCommand = append([]string(nil), cfg.Git.PostLandCommand...)
432				}
433				land, err := submit(req)
434				if err == nil {
435					submitted = land
436				}
437				return land, err
438			},
439		})
440		return submitted, outcome, landErr
441	}

449	// handlePostAttemptDecomposition runs the orchestrator-level splitter when a
450	// no_changes attempt signals orchestrator_action: decompose. It checks the
451	// queue-level max_decomposition_depth (not the implementation prompt cap),
452	// validates the AC map for completeness, and either creates children+deps or
453	// parks the parent for operator review if the split is lossy, depth-capped, or
454	// introduces a parent back-edge. The bead must already be unclaimed before
455	// this is called.
456	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handlePostAttemptDecomposition(ctx context.Context, candidate *bead.Bead, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, at time.Time) postAttemptDecompositionDecision {
457		decision := postAttemptDecompositionDecision{ExecutionDecision: "proposed"}
458		emit := func(kind string, body map[string]any) {
459			if runtime.EventSink == nil {
460				return
461			}
462			data, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{"event": kind, "payload": body})
463			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.EventSink, "%s\n", data)
464		}
465		parkOperator := func(reason string) {
466			if runtime.Log != nil {
467				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition blocked: %s (%s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
468			}
469			emit("post_attempt_decomposition.blocked", map[string]any{
470				"bead_id": candidate.ID,
471				"reason":  reason,
472			})
473			_, berr := parkBeadPostIntakeRejection(w.Store, candidate, assignee, PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, "operator_required", reason, at)
474			if berr != nil {
475				if runtime.Log != nil {
476					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition park error: %v\n", berr)
477				}
478			}
479		}
480	
481		// Queue-level depth check: orchestrator uses its own cap, not the
482		// implementation prompt's hardcoded depth-2 cap.
483		maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()
484		if maxDepth > 0 && storeBeadDepth(ctx, w.Store, candidate) >= maxDepth {
485			overflowBody, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
486				"depth": storeBeadDepth(ctx, w.Store, candidate),
487				"max":   maxDepth,
488			})
489			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
490				Kind:      "triage-overflow",
491				Summary:   "depth cap exceeded during post-attempt decomposition",
492				Body:      string(overflowBody),
493				Actor:     assignee,
494				Source:    "ddx work",
495				CreatedAt: at,
496			})
497			parkOperator("queue-level depth cap exceeded; operator must split")
498			return decision
499		}

21	// SidecarLivenessReporter is the production LivenessReporter. Each tick it
22	// (a) writes a status.json sidecar under .ddx/workers/<worker-id>/ via the
23	// workerstatus package, and (b) emits a worker.heartbeat envelope to sink
24	// (typically the loop EventSink, which is teed to the workerprobe). The
25	// sidecar write is best-effort and never fails the loop; the sink emit is
26	// best-effort and never blocks the heartbeat goroutine.
27	type SidecarLivenessReporter struct {
28		mu          sync.Mutex
29		projectRoot string
30		rec         workerstatus.LivenessRecord
31		sink        io.Writer

33	}

36	// given worker. projectRoot must be the project's git root (where .ddx/
37	// lives). sink may be nil for callers that don't want event mirroring.
38	func NewSidecarLivenessReporter(projectRoot, workerID, sessionID string, sink io.Writer) *SidecarLivenessReporter {
39		return &SidecarLivenessReporter{
40			projectRoot: projectRoot,
41			sink:        sink,
42			sessionID:   sessionID,
43			rec: workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
44				WorkerID:    workerID,
45				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
46				PID:         os.Getpid(),
47				StartedAt:   time.Now().UTC(),
48			},
49		}

113	// terminates. The worker_id and project_root remain so an idle drain loop
114	// still presents as the same worker between attempts.
115	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) ClearAttempt() {
116		if r == nil {
117			return
118		}
119		r.mu.Lock()
120		r.rec.CurrentBead = ""
121		r.rec.AttemptID = ""
122		r.rec.Phase = ""
123		r.rec.Message = ""
124		r.rec.Route = ""
125		r.rec.Harness = ""
126		r.rec.Model = ""
127		r.rec.Profile = ""
128		r.rec.ChildPID = 0
129		r.mu.Unlock()
130	}
131	
132	// OnTick updates the sidecar with the new last_activity_at and mirrors a
133	// worker.heartbeat envelope to the configured sink (best-effort).
134	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) OnTick(now time.Time) {
135		if r == nil {
136			return
137		}
138		r.mu.Lock()
139		r.rec.LastActivityAt = now.UTC()
140		snapshot := r.rec
141		r.mu.Unlock()
142		_ = workerstatus.WriteLiveness(r.projectRoot, snapshot.WorkerID, snapshot)
143		r.emit(snapshot, now)
144	}
145	
146	// Snapshot returns a copy of the current sidecar record.
147	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) Snapshot() workerstatus.LivenessRecord {
148		r.mu.Lock()
149		defer r.mu.Unlock()
150		return r.rec
151	}
152	
153	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) emit(rec workerstatus.LivenessRecord, now time.Time) {
154		if r.sink == nil {
155			return
156		}
157		entry := map[string]any{
158			"session_id": r.sessionID,
159			"type":       "worker.heartbeat",
160			"ts":         now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
161			"data": map[string]any{
162				"worker_id":        rec.WorkerID,
163				"bead_id":          rec.CurrentBead,
164				"attempt_id":       rec.AttemptID,
165				"phase":            rec.Phase,
166				"message":          rec.Message,
167				"route":            rec.Route,
168				"harness":          rec.Harness,
169				"model":            rec.Model,
170				"profile":          rec.Profile,
171				"last_activity_at": rec.LastActivityAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
172			},
173		}
174		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
175		if err != nil {
176			return
177		}
178		line = append(line, '\n')
179		_, _ = r.sink.Write(line)
180	}

10	// cmdSetProcessGroup configures cmd so that it and all its children share a
11	// new process group (Setpgid) and are automatically killed when the parent
12	// thread dies (Pdeathsig). This ensures that if the parent worker dies
13	// abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM), the harness child is reaped by the kernel
14	// rather than orphaning to PID 1.
15	//
16	// Pdeathsig is sent when the creating thread dies, not the process, so the
17	// caller must use runtime.LockOSThread around cmd.Start() to prevent Go
18	// runtime from migrating the goroutine to a different OS thread. See
19	// golang.org/issue/27505 for details.
20	//
21	// Note: Pdeathsig is Linux-only. macOS and Windows do not support it; the
22	// graceful shutdown path (cmdKillProcessGroup on context cancellation) serves
23	// as the fallback for those platforms, and the orphan reaper is the final
24	// backstop for already-dead workers.
25	func cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
26		cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{
27			Setpgid:   true,
28			Pdeathsig: syscall.SIGKILL,
29		}
30	}
31	
32	// cmdKillProcessGroup sends SIGKILL to the entire process group of cmd.
33	// Because cmd was started with Setpgid=true, its PID equals its PGID, so
34	// passing -pid kills all descendants that inherited the group.
35	func cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
36		if cmd.Process == nil {
37			return
38		}
39		_ = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
40	}

// TestWorkerStatusReportsProviderChildren proves `ddx work status --json`
// surfaces the per-attempt provider_children recorded in a fresh worker
// sidecar, including pid, provider/harness, route_owner, phase, and age, while
// the attempt is active. (ddx-c02ce93c AC3)
func TestWorkerStatusReportsProviderChildren(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	procStartedAt := time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Minute).UTC()
	const pid = 7676

	scannerWorkers := []workerstatus.LiveWorker{
		{
			PID:         pid,
			Command:     "ddx work --watch --project " + projectRoot,
			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
			StartedAt:   procStartedAt,
			Age:         "2m",
			AgeSeconds:  120,
		},
	}
	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, "worker-pc", workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:    "worker-pc",
		ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
		CurrentBead: "ddx-pc000001",
		AttemptID:   "20260613T202003-18b8bd7e",
		Phase:       "running",
		Route:       "claude/sonnet",
		Harness:     "claude",
		PID:         pid,
		StartedAt:   procStartedAt,
		ProviderChildren: []workerstatus.ProviderChild{
			{PID: 1882389, Provider: "claude", Harness: "claude", RouteOwner: "claude/sonnet", Phase: "running", AgeSeconds: 42},
		},
		LastActivityAt: time.Now().UTC(),
	}))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot)
	factory.workerScannerOverride = fixedScanner{workers: scannerWorkers}
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()

	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "status", "--project", projectRoot, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var report WorkStatusReport
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
	require.Len(t, report.Workers, 1)
	children := report.Workers[0].ProviderChildren
	require.Len(t, children, 1, "provider_children must be surfaced; got %s", out)
	c := children[0]
	assert.Equal(t, 1882389, c.PID)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude", c.Provider)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude", c.Harness)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude/sonnet", c.RouteOwner)
	assert.Equal(t, "running", c.Phase)
	assert.Equal(t, float64(42), c.AgeSeconds)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "provider_children", "raw JSON must carry the provider_children key")
}
EOF
